# willaicite

> willaicite is a free, deterministic audit that scores whether AI answer engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Claude) can retrieve a web page, extract it without executing JavaScript, and cite it in a synthesized answer. No LLM calls; deterministic.

## Pages

- [Home](https://willaicite.com/): what the audit tests and why SEO tooling misses it, including WAF/CDN bot blocking, no-JavaScript extraction, the outbound-citation inversion, per-engine crawler verdicts, liftable answers.
- [About](https://willaicite.com/about): who runs willaicite, how the audit works, evidence basis and contact.
- [AI crawler registry](https://willaicite.com/crawlers): every AI crawler token willaicite audits (OAI-SearchBot, GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot and more), retrieval vs training, and the robots.txt lines that control each.
- [Proof](https://willaicite.com/proof): the dogfood receipt, willaicite audited with willaicite, baseline 51/100 to 100/100 with every fix listed and reproducible; re-flagged and re-fixed under the stricter v1.3 scoring model.
- [Audit app](https://willaicite.com/audit): run a free deterministic audit of any URL.

## Evidence basis

- Aggarwal et al., "GEO: Generative Engine Optimization", KDD 2024: https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09735
- Vishwakarma et al., "What Gets Cited: Competitive GEO in AI Answer Engines", SIGIR 2026: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25517
- Puerto et al., "C-SEO Bench: Does Conversational SEO Work?", NeurIPS Datasets & Benchmarks 2025: https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.11097
- Kumar & Palkhouski, "AI Answer Engine Citation Behavior: The GEO-16 Framework", 2025: https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.10762
